ew_context: Price finding support at Fibonacci 61.8% retracement - typical wave 2 or wave B correction completion before next impulse leg
pattern_desc: Fibonacci 61.8% bounce pattern at 92.16 with bullish EMA structure and RSI above neutral confirming reversal momentum
invalidation: Close below 84.37 on D1 timeframe would break the swing low and invalidate the bounce pattern
market_context: WTI supported by Middle East tensions and dollar weakness as US-Iran deal hopes create short-term volatility
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

WTI Crude has established support at the Fibonacci 61.8% retracement level at 92.16, creating a high-probability bounce setup after the decline from 117.63 highs. The EMA20 at 98.87 above EMA50 at 91.99 confirms the underlying bullish trend structure remains intact. This Fibonacci level coincides with prior support around the 91.05 swing low, adding confluence to the reversal zone.

RSI at 55.9% shows constructive momentum above the midpoint, while the 6.91 ATR reflects normal volatility conditions. The laminar flow with aligned momentum supports the bullish thesis.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 99.33 (Fibonacci 38.2% retracement and initial target)
- **Support**: 92.16 (current Fibonacci 61.8% bounce level)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 84.37 on D1 timeframe would break below the swing low structure and negate the Fibonacci bounce pattern.

## Macro & Flow Context

Middle East geopolitical developments and broad dollar weakness are providing fundamental tailwinds for crude oil. CFD flow shows Reynolds number of 17.67 with momentum alignment, indicating institutional accumulation at these technical levels.
